channel.title: Microbiome Digest
channel.description: Daily automated digest of microbiome research
channel.itunes:author: Veronica Llorens
channel.itunes:summary: Daily automated digest of microbiome research

item.title: Daily Podcast 2026-03-22
item.description: Centrohelid heliozoans are a group of predatory protists that use stiff radiating arms to capture bacteria, algae, and other small organisms in aquatic and soil environments. | A new study has used full-length 16S rRNA gene amplicon sequencing with Pacific Biosciences technology to characterize the bacterial communities associated with seven isolates of centrohelid heliozoans from freshwater habitats. | The sequencing revealed a remarkably diverse bacterial microbiome spanning five phyla, six classes, and 58 genera.
item.itunes:author: Veronica Llorens
item.itunes:summary: Centrohelid heliozoans are a group of predatory protists that use stiff radiating arms to capture bacteria, algae, and other small organisms in aquatic and soil environments. | A new study has used full-length 16S rRNA gene amplicon sequencing with Pacific Biosciences technology to characterize the bacterial communities associated with seven isolates of centrohelid heliozoans from freshwater habitats. | The sequencing revealed a remarkably diverse bacterial microbiome spanning five phyla, six classes, and 58 genera.
